cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The question is about a study by the World Bank in 1974 regarding economic growth and development in some backward countries. The passage says that in the early 1970s, the relationship between growth and development was being questioned, and the 1974 World Bank study concluded that "more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, that wording is a bit tricky. Let me parse it again. The exact sentence is: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So, the study found that even after many years of rapid growth, only a third of the population (so, about 33%) had benefited. That means the growth didn't actually benefit most of the population.
